event: presented to the hospital with complaints of abdominal distension
computed tomography scan of his abdomen
mural thickening of the terminal ileum
abdominal granulomatous infection or neoplastic process suggested
abnormal CD4/CD8 double-negative T-cell population (38%) with multiple phenotypic aberrancies
high-grade peripheral T-cell lymphoma (PTCL), not other specified, confirmed
staged as a stage IV lymphoma
transfer to the medical intensive care unit (ICU) for severe sepsis
stayed in ICU care for around 1 week
transferred to the national cancer center
commenced on the EPOCH chemotherapy protocol
power was decreased in all proximal and distal muscles (3/5)
normal sensory nerve action potential amplitude
repetitive nerve stimulation (30–40 Hz) showed a significant incremental response
VGCC antibodies not done because of technical issues
significant improvement of his motor function
complete metabolic remission by positron emission tomography/computed tomography
planned for consolidation by autologous bone marrow transplant
re-admitted to the medical ICU for severe sepsis and multiorgan failure
